description_p1: CJC-1295 with DAC is a synthetic analog of human growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH) (1–29) that incorporates a drug affinity complex (DAC) moiety to enable covalent binding to serum albumin. The peptide backbone is a modified GHRH(1–29) sequence (e.g., Tyr‑D‑Ala‑Asp‑Ala‑Ile‑Phe‑Thr‑Gln‑Ser‑Tyr‑Arg‑Lys‑Val‑Leu‑Ala‑Gln‑Leu‑Ser‑Ala‑Arg‑Lys‑Leu‑…) with empirical formula C165H269N47O46 reported for the DAC-conjugated form. The DAC group is typically attached via a lysine side chain to form a stable albumin-reactive entity.
description_p2: CJC-1295 DAC is used as a long-acting GHRH analog in in‑vitro and in‑vivo models examining class B GHRH receptor pharmacology and growth hormone secretagogue signalling. It serves as a reference compound in studies of receptor binding, cAMP generation, and pharmacokinetic extension via albumin bioconjugation.
description_p3: Research-grade CJC-1295 DAC is supplied as a lyophilized peptide at ≥98% purity by HPLC, typically as an acetate or trifluoroacetate salt. The material is reconstituted in sterile water or aqueous buffer immediately prior to use and stored lyophilized at −20°C, with reconstituted aliquots kept frozen and protected from light. Reference standard for in-vitro research on GHRH receptor signalling and albumin bioconjugation strategies. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
sequence: Modified GHRH(1–29) peptide with albumin-binding DAC moiety
seo_title: CJC-1295 DAC Reference Standard | GHRH Analog
seo_description: Long-acting GHRH(1–29) analog with DAC for in-vitro studies of growth hormone secretagogue receptor signalling. ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the structural relationship between CJC-1295 DAC and native GHRH?
faq_1_a: CJC-1295 DAC is based on the 1–29 N-terminal fragment of human GHRH with sequence modifications and an added drug affinity complex that enables albumin binding.
faq_2_q: Why is the DAC group incorporated into CJC-1295?
faq_2_a: The DAC moiety forms covalent or quasi-covalent bonds with circulating albumin, extending the peptide's residence time and enabling long-acting receptor engagement in preclinical models.
faq_3_q: How is CJC-1295 DAC typically formulated for research?
faq_3_a: Vendors supply CJC-1295 DAC as a lyophilized, high-purity peptide salt intended for reconstitution in aqueous media for biochemical and cell-based assays.
ref_1: Wikipedia. CJC-1295 – long-acting GHRH analog with DAC.
ref_2: Particle Peptides safety data sheet for CJC-1295 + DAC, listing CAS 863288-34-0 and peptide formula.

description_p1: Ipamorelin is a synthetic pentapeptide with the sequence Aib‑His‑D‑2‑Nal‑D‑Phe‑Lys‑NH2, where Aib is α‑aminoisobutyric acid and 2‑Nal is 3‑(2‑naphthyl)‑D‑alanine. It has the empirical formula C38H49N9O5 and a molecular weight of approximately 711.85 Da. Ipamorelin is a highly selective agonist of the ghrelin/growth hormone secretagogue receptor (GHSR‑1a) and belongs to the class of non‑endogenous growth hormone secretagogues.
description_p2: In research, ipamorelin is widely used as a reference GHSR agonist in in‑vitro assays and ex‑vivo pituitary or hypothalamic systems. It is applied in binding, calcium-flux, and cAMP assays, as well as in perfused organ and animal models studying growth hormone secretagogue receptor signalling.
description_p3: Research-grade ipamorelin is provided mainly as the acetate salt in lyophilized form with purity ≥98% by HPLC. It shows good stability when stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture, and is typically reconstituted in water, dilute acids, or mixed aqueous–organic solvents for experimental use. Reference standard for in-vitro research on ghrelin/growth hormone secretagogue receptor pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_description: Pentapeptide GHSR agonist (Aib-His-D-2-Nal-D-Phe-Lys-NH2) for in-vitro growth hormone secretagogue receptor research. ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What receptor does ipamorelin target in research models?
faq_1_a: Ipamorelin is a selective agonist at the ghrelin/growth hormone secretagogue receptor GHSR‑1a and is used to study this receptor's signalling in vitro.
faq_2_q: What is the amino acid composition of ipamorelin?
faq_2_a: It is a pentapeptide composed of Aib, His, D‑2‑Nal, D‑Phe, and Lys, with a C‑terminal amide.
faq_3_q: How is ipamorelin typically supplied for laboratory use?
faq_3_a: Vendors offer ipamorelin predominantly as a lyophilized acetate salt with ≥98% HPLC purity, intended for reconstitution immediately before experiments.
ref_1: ChemicalBook entry for Ipamorelin, CAS 170851-70-4, including formula and sequence.
ref_2: CymitQuimica technical description of Ipamorelin, listing formula and molecular weight.

description_p1: 5-Amino-1MQ is a synthetic small-molecule research probe belonging to the quaternary quinolinium class. It is commonly referenced by the cation name 5-amino-1-methylquinolinium (C10H11N2+) and is frequently supplied as an iodide salt (C10H11IN2) with CAS number 42464-96-0. The compound has been discussed in preclinical literature as a substrate-site–targeting inhibitor candidate for nicotinamide N‑methyltransferase (NNMT).
description_p2: In vitro, 5-Amino-1MQ is used as a reference NNMT-modulating small molecule in enzymatic assays, metabolite profiling workflows, and pathway studies assessing methylation-related biochemical readouts. It is applied in cell-free and cell-based systems interrogating NNMT activity and downstream metabolite changes in controlled experimental settings.
description_p3: Research-grade 5-Amino-1MQ iodide is typically supplied as a solid powder with ≥98% HPLC purity. It is soluble in polar solvents such as water and DMSO, and stability data and catalog guidance recommend storage at −20°C in a dry environment to preserve integrity. Reference standard for in-vitro research on NNMT-associated biochemical pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_description: Quinolinium NNMT research probe (5-Amino-1-methylquinolinium iodide) for in-vitro nicotinamide N-methyltransferase pathway studies. ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the preferred chemical form of 5-Amino-1MQ for research use?
faq_1_a: Many catalogs provide the iodide salt of 5-amino-1-methylquinolinium, which offers defined composition and good aqueous solubility.
faq_2_q: What enzyme system is commonly interrogated with 5-Amino-1MQ?
faq_2_a: 5-Amino-1MQ is used in in‑vitro assays of nicotinamide N‑methyltransferase (NNMT) to explore substrate-site modulation and downstream metabolite shifts.
faq_3_q: Are there public database entries for 5-Amino-1MQ?
faq_3_a: Yes. The cation and its salt forms appear in PubChem (e.g., CID 950107) and commercial chemical databases with structural and analytical data.
ref_1: BlkcellResearch technical monograph for 5-Amino-1MQ, summarizing NNMT probe usage and identifiers.

description_p1: NAD+ (nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide, oxidized form) is a ubiquitous pyridine nucleotide cofactor composed of an adenosine diphosphate ribose moiety linked via pyrophosphate to nicotinamide riboside. The oxidized species has empirical formula C21H26N7O14P2− (for the anionic form) and a molecular weight around 662.4 g/mol. NAD+ participates in redox reactions as an electron acceptor and is central to dehydrogenase-catalyzed metabolic transformations in biochemical systems.
description_p2: In research, NAD+ is used as a reference cofactor in enzyme-kinetic assays, mitochondrial function studies, and in‑vitro models of energy metabolism. It is routinely employed in spectrophotometric dehydrogenase assays, sirtuin activity measurements, and other in‑vitro systems that require defined pyridine nucleotide pools.
description_p3: Research-grade NAD+ is typically supplied as the disodium or free acid salt in lyophilized powder form with ≥98% purity. It is highly water-soluble and generally stored at −20°C in sealed containers protected from moisture and repeated freeze–thaw; reconstituted solutions are kept refrigerated or frozen depending on experimental timelines. Reference standard for in-vitro research on redox biochemistry and pyridine-nucleotide–dependent metabolic pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
storage: -20°C; dry; protect from moisture and light
seo_description: Oxidized nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide cofactor for in-vitro redox enzyme and metabolic pathway studies. Lyophilized, ≥98% purity. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the primary chemical role of NAD+ in research assays?
faq_1_a: NAD+ acts as an oxidizing cofactor, accepting hydride equivalents in dehydrogenase and related enzyme reactions during in‑vitro kinetic studies.
faq_2_q: In which assay formats is NAD+ most frequently used?
faq_2_a: It is a standard component in spectrophotometric dehydrogenase assays, mitochondrial respiration studies, and diverse enzyme systems requiring defined pyridine nucleotide concentrations.
faq_3_q: How is research-grade NAD+ typically formulated and stored?
faq_3_a: Vendors supply NAD+ as a lyophilized powder that is reconstituted with water immediately before use and stored at low temperature in the dark to limit degradation.
ref_1: PubChem CID 15938971. Nad+. National Center for Biotechnology Information.
ref_2: PubChem entries for nicotinamide–adenine dinucleotide and Nadide.

description_p1: Semax is a synthetic heptapeptide analogue of the adrenocorticotropic hormone fragment ACTH(4–10) with the sequence Met‑Glu‑His‑Phe‑Pro‑Gly‑Pro (MEHFPGP). It preserves the core ACTH(4–7) sequence and incorporates an additional Pro‑Gly‑Pro motif at the C‑terminus. The peptide has been characterized as a regulatory neuropeptide analog with high affinity for copper(II) ions in solution.
description_p2: Semax is used as a reference ACTH-derived peptide in in‑vitro and ex‑vivo models investigating neuropeptide signalling, neurotrophin expression, and metal-ion–associated oxidative processes. It has been studied in cell cultures for its interactions with copper(II) and for effects on viability under metal-induced stress conditions.
description_p3: Research-grade semax is supplied as a lyophilized acetate salt at ≥98% HPLC purity for experimental work. It is water-soluble and generally stored at −20°C protected from light, with reconstituted solutions maintained at low temperature and used promptly. Reference standard for in-vitro research on ACTH-fragment neuropeptides, metal–peptide interactions, and neurotrophin-related pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: Semax Reference Standard | ACTH Fragment Peptide
seo_description: Heptapeptide ACTH(4–10) analog (MEHFPGP) for in-vitro neuropeptide and metal–peptide signalling research. Lyophilized, ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the sequence origin of Semax?
faq_1_a: Semax is derived from the ACTH(4–10) fragment and extended with a C‑terminal Pro‑Gly‑Pro motif, yielding the heptapeptide MEHFPGP.
faq_2_q: Which experimental systems commonly employ Semax?
faq_2_a: It is used in neuronal and glial cell models, as well as biochemical systems, to study neuropeptide signalling and copper(II)-associated oxidative stress responses.
faq_3_q: Does Semax form complexes with metal ions?
faq_3_a: Yes. A detailed study reported high-affinity complexation of Semax with Cu(II) in solution, associated with protective effects in metal-induced toxicity models.
ref_1: Wikipedia. Semax – ACTH(4–10) analog with sequence MEHFPGP.
ref_2: Palacios C. et al., 2015. Semax, an ACTH 4–10 peptide analog with high affinity for copper(II) ion and protective ability against metal-induced cell toxicity. J Inorg Biochem.

description_p1: Selank is a synthetic heptapeptide with the sequence Thr‑Lys‑Pro‑Arg‑Pro‑Gly‑Pro (TKPRPGP), designed as an analog of the endogenous tetrapeptide tuftsin (Thr‑Lys‑Pro‑Arg). The C‑terminal Pro‑Gly‑Pro extension enhances peptide stability while preserving the tuftsin motif. Selank is classified as a regulatory neuropeptide analog in the Russian peptide literature.
description_p2: Selank is used as a reference peptide in in‑vitro and animal models assessing neuropeptide signalling, synaptic plasticity markers, and neurotrophin expression. It has been examined in systems measuring brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) expression and in receptor-binding or gene-expression assays relevant to stress-response pathways.
description_p3: Research-grade Selank is supplied as a lyophilized acetate salt with typical purity ≥98% by HPLC. It is soluble in water and aqueous buffers and is stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture; reconstituted solutions are kept refrigerated or frozen according to experimental schedules. Reference standard for in-vitro research on tuftsin-related neuropeptides and BDNF-associated signalling pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: Selank Reference Standard | Tuftsin Analog Peptide
seo_description: Heptapeptide tuftsin analog (TKPRPGP) for in-vitro neuropeptide and BDNF-pathway research. Lyophilized acetate salt, ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What structural motif does Selank contain?
faq_1_a: Selank contains the tuftsin tetrapeptide Thr‑Lys‑Pro‑Arg extended by Pro‑Gly‑Pro at the C‑terminus, improving stability while preserving the core motif.
faq_2_q: What research areas commonly employ Selank?
faq_2_a: It is used in neurobiology models studying neuropeptide signalling, BDNF expression, and gene-expression changes associated with stress-response pathways.
faq_3_a: Vendors provide Selank as a lyophilized acetate salt of ≥98% purity, intended for reconstitution in water or buffer prior to in‑vitro or preclinical use.
ref_1: Wikipedia. Selank – heptapeptide tuftsin analog TKPRPGP.
ref_2: ChemicalBook entry for Selank peptide, CAS 129954-34-3.

description_p1: Kisspeptin-10 (KP‑10) is a decapeptide fragment of the human KISS1 gene product, corresponding to residues 45–54 (Metastin 45–54) with the sequence Tyr‑Asn‑Trp‑Asn‑Ser‑Phe‑Gly‑Leu‑Arg‑Phe‑NH2 (YNWNSFGLRF‑NH2). It has the empirical formula C63H83N17O14 and a molecular weight of approximately 1302.4 g/mol. Kisspeptin‑10 is a high-affinity ligand for the G protein–coupled receptor KISS1R (GPR54), which plays a key role in the hypothalamic–pituitary–gonadal axis.
description_p2: In vitro, kisspeptin‑10 is widely used as a reference peptide in receptor-binding, calcium-mobilization, and cAMP signalling assays in KISS1R‑expressing cell systems. It is also applied in pituitary and hypothalamic models exploring GnRH neuron activation and downstream gonadotropin-related pathways.
description_p3: Research-grade kisspeptin‑10 is supplied as a lyophilized acetate or trifluoroacetate salt with ≥98% HPLC purity. It is soluble in water and dilute acetic acid, and is stored at −20°C protected from light; once reconstituted, aliquots are typically frozen to maintain peptide stability. Reference standard for in-vitro research on KISS1R signalling and hypothalamic–pituitary–gonadal axis regulation. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_description: Decapeptide KISS1R ligand (YNWNSFGLRF-NH2) for in-vitro GnRH neuron and hypothalamic–pituitary–gonadal signalling research. ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the sequence of kisspeptin‑10?
faq_2_q: Which receptor does kisspeptin‑10 activate in research systems?
faq_2_a: Kisspeptin‑10 is a high-affinity agonist of KISS1R (GPR54) and is commonly used to probe this receptor's signalling in vitro.
faq_3_q: How is kisspeptin‑10 typically used in endocrine research?
faq_3_a: It is employed in pituitary and hypothalamic models to study GnRH neuron activation and related hypothalamic–pituitary–gonadal axis signalling.
ref_1: PubChem CID 25240297. Kisspeptin-10. National Center for Biotechnology Information.
ref_2: Dhillo W.S. et al., 2011. Kisspeptin‑10 is a potent stimulator of LH and increases pulse frequency. J Clin Endocrinol Metab.

description_p1: Oxytocin is a cyclic nonapeptide hormone and neuropeptide with the sequence Cys‑Tyr‑Ile‑Gln‑Asn‑Cys‑Pro‑Leu‑Gly‑NH2 (CYIQNCPLG‑NH2), featuring a disulfide bridge between Cys1 and Cys6. It has the empirical formula C43H66N12O12S2 and is classified as a member of the vasopressin/oxytocin peptide family. Oxytocin acts via the oxytocin receptor (OXTR), a class A GPCR.
description_p2: In research, oxytocin is used as a reference ligand in receptor-binding and functional assays investigating OXTR-mediated G‑protein signalling, intracellular calcium flux, and β‑arrestin recruitment. It is also applied in myometrial strip preparations, neuronal cultures, and ex‑vivo tissue systems to characterize oxytocin receptor pharmacology and downstream signalling cascades.
description_p3: Research-grade oxytocin is supplied as a lyophilized peptide, often as an acetate salt, with ≥98% HPLC purity. It is readily soluble in water and dilute acids, and is stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture; reconstituted solutions are typically kept refrigerated or frozen and used within defined stability windows. Reference standard for in-vitro research on oxytocin receptor signalling and vasopressin/oxytocin-family neuropeptides. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_description: Cyclic nonapeptide hormone (CYIQNCPLG-NH2) for in-vitro oxytocin receptor and vasopressin-family signalling research. Lyophilized, ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the primary receptor target of oxytocin?
faq_1_a: Oxytocin is an endogenous ligand for the oxytocin receptor (OXTR), a G protein–coupled receptor studied in many biochemical and tissue-based assays.
faq_2_q: What structural feature stabilizes oxytocin's conformation?
faq_2_a: A disulfide bridge between residues Cys1 and Cys6 cyclizes the peptide backbone, constraining its conformation.
faq_3_q: How is oxytocin typically supplied for experimental use?
faq_3_a: It is sold as a lyophilized peptide of high purity, commonly as the acetate salt, for reconstitution in aqueous media before in‑vitro or ex‑vivo experiments.
ref_2: CymitQuimica entry for oxytocin, CAS 50-56-6, with structural description.

description_p1: LL‑37 is a 37‑amino‑acid cationic α‑helical peptide generated by proteolytic processing of the human cathelicidin precursor hCAP‑18. Its sequence is LLGDFFRKSKEKIGKEFKRIVQRIKDFLRNLVPRTES, and it carries a net positive charge under physiological conditions. LL‑37 is the only human cathelicidin identified to date and is classified as an antimicrobial host-defense peptide.
description_p2: In research, LL‑37 is widely used as a reference antimicrobial peptide in in‑vitro assays evaluating bactericidal activity, membrane permeabilization, and peptide–lipid interactions. It is also applied in studies of innate immune signalling, nucleic-acid complex formation, and cytokine induction in various cell types.
description_p3: Research-grade LL‑37 is typically supplied as a lyophilized trifluoroacetate or acetate salt at ≥95–98% HPLC purity. It is soluble in water and aqueous buffers, especially at slightly acidic pH, and is stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture, with reconstituted aliquots maintained frozen to preserve activity. Reference standard for in-vitro research on antimicrobial host-defense peptides, membrane interactions, and innate immune signalling pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: LL-37 Reference Standard | Human Cathelicidin Peptide
seo_description: Human cathelicidin peptide (37 aa) for in-vitro antimicrobial, membrane-interaction, and innate immune signalling research. Lyophilized. RUO.
faq_1_a: LL‑37 is derived from the C‑terminal region of the human cathelicidin precursor hCAP‑18 by proteolytic processing.
faq_2_q: Which in-vitro assays commonly employ LL‑37?
faq_2_a: It is used in bacterial killing assays, liposome leakage experiments, and cell-based studies dissecting innate immune signalling and nucleic-acid complexation.
faq_3_q: How is LL‑37 typically formulated for laboratory use?
faq_3_a: Vendors supply LL‑37 as a lyophilized peptide salt of high purity that is reconstituted in water or buffer immediately before antimicrobial and mechanistic assays.
ref_1: Wikipedia. Cathelicidin antimicrobial peptide – LL‑37 overview.
ref_2: Nijnik A. et al., 2013. The human cathelicidin LL-37 as a multifunctional peptide in infection and inflammation. Expert Rev Anti Infect Ther.

description_p1: KPV is a tripeptide corresponding to residues 11–13 of α‑melanocyte-stimulating hormone (α‑MSH) with the sequence Lys‑Pro‑Val (H‑Lys‑Pro‑Val‑OH). It has the empirical formula C16H30N4O4 and a molecular weight of approximately 342.43 g/mol. KPV retains the C‑terminal motif of α‑MSH and is categorized as a melanocortin-related peptide fragment in the experimental peptide literature.
description_p2: In research, KPV is used as a reference peptide in in‑vitro models studying melanocortin receptor signalling and inflammatory pathway markers. It has been examined in cell and tissue systems for its effects on cytokine readouts, NF‑κB pathway components, and epithelial barrier-associated parameters in controlled experimental contexts.
description_p3: Research-grade KPV is supplied as a lyophilized peptide at ≥95–98% HPLC purity. It is highly hydrophilic and soluble in water and aqueous buffers, and is stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture; reconstituted solutions are typically aliquoted and frozen to maintain stability. Reference standard for in-vitro research on melanocortin-related peptides and inflammatory-signalling pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_description: α-MSH(11–13) fragment (Lys-Pro-Val) for in-vitro melanocortin receptor and inflammatory-signalling research. Lyophilized peptide, ≥95–98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the structural origin of KPV?
faq_1_a: KPV corresponds to the C‑terminal three residues (11–13) of α‑MSH and represents a minimal melanocortin-related peptide fragment.
faq_2_q: Which research areas commonly employ KPV?
faq_2_a: It is used in studies of melanocortin receptor engagement and in in‑vitro models assessing inflammatory-signalling markers and epithelial responses.
faq_3_q: How is KPV typically supplied and stored?
faq_3_a: Vendors provide KPV as a synthetic, lyophilized tripeptide with high HPLC purity, stored at −20°C and reconstituted in aqueous media before experiments.
ref_1: Getting S.J. et al., 2004. α‑MSH tripeptide analogs activate melanocortin 1 receptor and modulate inflammatory responses. J Pharmacol Exp Ther.
ref_2: NovoPro Bioscience product sheet for α‑MSH(11–13)/KPV, including sequence and analytical data.
